item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scip just didn't click with me for some reason. That said having seen the amount of effort and care put in by the author I feel it deserves to go from a no vote to a +1. I appreciate a well thought out and reworked scip even if it doesn't resonate with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an old comment, but it feeds into my ultimate thoughts on the skip.

The SCP is supposed to be repulsive

"It's supposed to be repulsive" doesn't work if the narrative doesn't present it as repulsive, and 1299 doesn't feel like it's presenting it as repulsive. At least to me, the violence presented in 1299 feels almost voyeuristic, a culmination of contrivances meant to justify a constant stream of nameless dead for the viewer's entertainment.

The actual history, what little we learn, consist of two incredibly brief recounts of "a man drowned me in a tub" and an out of the blue "yeah i remember that thing it killed my wife". We don't even really get names; we're left with noncharacters servicing the central premise, the narrative winking through a thin veneer of "we sure do live in a society, huh".

Despite its reputation, exploitation media requires an immense amount of care to rise beyond even just mediocrity. Even the likes of Cannibal Holocaust1 or Postal 12 had to be careful in how they handled everything.

And ultimately, this isn't even about """"politics in muh writing""""3, it's about writing something people won't tune out the second it's out of memory.

i'd like to thank my computer for not crashing me as i wrote this

Footnotes
1. A critique of sensationalist news media and neocolonialism.
2. A critique of trivial moral panic
3. A complaint, distressingly common enough that I'm forced to acknowledge it in these matters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The soul exchange between bodies of effected humans reminds me of SCP-022

item.title: Re: Great read
item.content:encoded: Hey just happened to be checking back and saw this. No other bathtub would manifest the qualities of 1299, it's specifically tied to that bathtub. And yes the city would be at stake, hence the story with the D-class. But who's going to believe that a bathtub drowned someone rather than it was an accident or a murder?

item.title: Re: Great read
item.content:encoded: I originally said SCP-079. I double checked and changed it to SCP-072. It's hard to memorize numbers based off of only the name of the SCP. Especially when there are over 2000 of them

item.title: Great read
item.content:encoded: It looks like the description of the SCP is great and the tests and interviews behind the story gets you thinking about the description. I just thought of a different question: Let's say SCP-1299 was stolen somehow. I know this would never happen but assume it does. If every 26th of the month, SCP-1299-1 drowns a random person, wouldn't any local city be at stake? Also, is this SCP like SCP-072 where if any instance of SCP-1299 touches another bathtub, said bathtub manifests the qualities of SCP-1299? I am really interested to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would really love to see this added to SCP CB. I always liked this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I really like the fact that one of the D class actually got arrested for a crime committed by an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, the Foundation being cruel, I missed this kind of scips. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test log was good, but I do take a slight issue at the fact that the description doesn't indicate that this thing must kill/possess people for it to be appeased, just that the handle must be turned. I get that there's maybe a technicality by which it really cannot be stopped if even started to be summoned, but without that being addressed, it seems like the Foundation didn't try to trick it.

I mean, contrast it with the chocolate fountain: there's clear cut reasons why that thing is being constantly fed people. Here it's "eh, better be safe, though we don't fully know".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We've proven in the past that Class D's can get a indef suspension of termination or promotion to something less… well… disposable.

The foundation has a D that's innocent. Moral grey area aside, I don't think they'd ice him when they could just as easily put him somewhere moderately useful.

If junior researchers can be demoted to class D, no reason why it shouldn't work the other way around.

+1 withheld for nao. no downvote because I liked the concept of murdertub.scp, but the last bit is leaving me on the fence.

item.title: I still don't see why
item.content:encoded:
I still don't see why they couldn't just make the guy into a janitor for the facility or something, instead of keeping him as a D-Class scheduled to be killed by the end of the month when they now know damn well that he's innocent…

I'm down-voting the scip for that reason. I understand that the Foundation is supposed to be "morally gray" and all that, but I'm honestly sick of the whole "LOL LET'S HAVE THE FOUNDATION DO SOMETHING ABSOLUTELY AWFUL JUST BECAUSE IT'S FUNNY LOLFOUNDATION!" thing.

My point is, they have countless options available where they could take this guy off the D-Class roster and not have to kill him, but at the same time wouldn't have to release him back into the general public and cause questions to be raised as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The collapsible logs are mislabeled. Interview 1299-A is displayed as 1229 until expanded and Interview 1299-B is displayed as 1229 both collapsed and expanded.

Aside from that, I wonder about the containment procedures. It's mentioned that Procedure F-30 doesn't activate while SCP-1299 is full of bleach, so why not keep it that way to prevent unnecessary D Class use.

I was also curious if the thought pattern replacement caused a certain subject to repeatedly relive the drowning.

As someone who bathes regularly, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, so I haven't been on the site for a while but unless the general idea has changed, the Foundation doesn't really neutralize SCPs just because they kill people. They contain to try make the objects are predictable as possible and research them. Besides, depending on what non-canon canon you believe, most of the D-class end up disposed of or dead at some point.

Side note, The SCP is supposed to be repulsive, it kills females in a brutal almost "domestic violence-y" way. That's not meant to be a good thing, it just is a fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is repugnant.

any available female D-class are to be escorted towards the breach site by security.

Something about that just really makes me incredibly irritated and repulsed by this SCP. Why are we letting it murder people? This is exactly the sort of SCP that should be neutralized — it doesn't do anything other than randomly murder people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last log made me upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is that he isn't the murderer, the tub had its effects before he ever had it, he was a witness to the tub, everything he said was true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: would like it a ton better if it were written more well. as it is it just seems like a generic "it kills things" kinda thing to me :\ and i did not get the impression that that d class was the murderer at all. especially since how would he be able to say nealry its exact effects. seems kind of a stretch to say he made it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could this possibly be Keter? No matter the containment procedures, an invisible force will leave and drown a random person if it is not "fed" consistently.
I liked it though, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: upvoted because a D-class survives at the end! hahaha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much thanks, thats basically what i was trying to make the reader think, haha. Is the tub responsible for the murders or are the people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I thought that this is some kind of a bathtub haunted by the killer. But then when the D class person that seemed responsible for the murder shown up, the SCP really became mysterious (as in, did the D-class do the murder? But why is there a murdering figure in the SCP? Or was he just a victim?). I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks mate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one—really creeped me out. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clickety-click!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh if you can get the original again i'd love it, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The original file you had is now up, but I haven't changed it to point at it.

Up to you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed image to cope

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is. Offsite images often break. You can use them, but this happens a LOT when people link from sites they don't own.

I'll see if I can fix it this evening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the language can be tightened up in places, but the test logs definitely secured you an upvote. I like my SCPs with a little tragedy on the side alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image seems to be broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for feedback, the identity of how this SCP came to be as with most other SCPs is a mystery, the ambiguous part about it all is whether or not in the various cases it -was- actually the boyfriend/husband/etc. Inferred by the reaction of the D-class at the end you can say that in at least one case the tub was the murderer, but i'll leave you with this.

Who's to say the tub isn't just imprinting the consciousness of random domestic drowning victims, regardless of whether they were in the tub at the time or not? No one ever said the imprinted personalities all died in this exact tub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It should be more definitive that the original murder was actually done by the boyfriend, not the tub. Saying that he was convicted on circumstantial evidence is a little weak, given the fate of the D-class later on.

And the original murder was done by the boyfriend, because that's why the tub reactivates at that time and uses the consciousness of the original victim.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, if only because I quite enjoy SCPs where the method of containment is even more creepy than the SCP itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remembering there is no canon:

You can't just firebomb towns over and over - people would eventually notice. So they hope and pray amnesiacs hold (I think there's a story somewhere where it didn't, or someone was immune or something) and no one catches on to the discrepancies. Old timers think it used to be SO much easier in the old days when you didn't have to go through EVERY electronic record to make them match up. Why in my day…

So they use it on witnesses as fast as possible or to people who never really figure out WHO they are talking about. Lack of Canon says that it works as long Dramatic Effect says it doesn't.

Regardless - this is a prisoner they ALREADY made disappear. They have him in custody. Doing anything ELSE would just raise more questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Then why does the Foundation have amnesiacs?

Besides, they have to know that their secrecy will come crashing down soon enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the end, you're missing the most important point:

It's a story. It was written by a person. That person chose the ending. If we don't like it (there are other stories where the Foundation does far, FAR worse to innocent bystanders, FYI) then we can gripe about it (done), ignore it, downvote it, and/or write something else.

I'm not Staff (obviously) but don't forget that we operate under the Creative Commons - the work is still owned by the author. Because it's Anonymouse99 choice of changing it or not.

Right now the class-D has 30 days to live - and that would matter if he was anymore then a few lines of description.

As for the characterization, this is the place that allowed a second child to be eaten by 682. They will do ANYTHING the think is necessary, because they know if they don't the consequences defy imagination. These are people that Churchill got off easy whether or not he knew. Because killing one innocent to maybe save the world should NOT be a hard choice; because if one person blabbed about the Foundation, the entire secret of trying to save everyone from the menace of EVERYTHING goes out the window.

The Foundation coming out of the dark means MC&D get a website and start selling ANYTHING, real or not. The Chaos Insurgency gets it's followers from PETA so 682 can be released "safely" into the wild; the Church of the Broken God gets to claim religious persecution and opens up temples in broad daylight and people would FLOCK because they can do miracles! Prometheus Labs would IPO and get billions for R&D in the first day. Doctor Wondertainment knockoffs would be sold at Spencer's Gifts at the mall.

All because someone wanted to give a class-D a break. Yea, he was innocent.

Tough break.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand what you're saying, but i think that's kind of an unfair assumption. As is repeated so many times "There is no canon." There are tales and SCPs where the foundation seems like they could give 0 damns about anyone else, and others where it really comes to light that they "do everything they can for humanity" The Foundation isn't black and white, and many could argue about it's true intentions (I mean the GOC are the real "good guys" if you wanna go that way) But yeah i honestly do get what you're saying.

However in my opinion at least, the Foundation wouldn't use its resources to set up an entirely new life for someone, new identity, etc. and risk a relapse despite amnesiacs, or risk having someone recognize him, when it could put him back in D-class.

Edit: This was a reply to Plilt, not Photo, sorry :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're entitled to your opinion, but I for one disagree. Releasing the poor sap was jarringly out-of-genre for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Removing the D-Class being released after given amnesiacs does not make me like this any more. It makes me like it much, much less. The Foundation isn’t fluffy kittens, but that doesn’t mean that after finding out someone was wrongfully convicted there’s no chance that they’ll be let go. It’s not going to happen often, especially since the Foundation probably wouldn’t put much time into seeing if they were innocent or not. But this is an excellent place where one could subvert the expectations of the Foundation never having any mercy.

That kind of thing gets done all the time, with the Foundation being Officially Dark and Gritty all the time, and it’s easy to write a medicore entry where the Foundation is Officially Dark and Gritty. But this was a very easy place to insert an example of the Foundation doing the Right Thing, which made it unusual and therefore, in my eyes, a great deal better.

The Foundation is made up of humans, and most aren’t sociopathic or you’d have splinter cells left and right. Stands to reason that every now and then, when a D-Class is found to have been wrongfully convicted by means of an SCP, they’ll be released if there’s nothing else the Foundation can get out of them.

Or promoted to a janitor so they don’t die at the end of the month (which is, of course, inane anyway).

Therefore, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last log definitely falls under the "fate worse than death" category for me. Although, given Foundation protocols, not for very long…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. That should have been obvious, I suppose. So is the water then from the place the tub (or, I'd assume, the murder) originated, the place it is currently, or does it have nothing to do with anything? I don't think it's necessary to specify in the article, but I am curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm good point guys, i'll remove humanoid references.

Edit: looking back i can't see anywhere where i designated it more then an "unknown force", i feel like it's footprints, partially-corporeal nature is enough to at least admit that something is there.

Though if you guys have an idea as to what you're saying (i'm a bit confused on it honestly) feel free to make a change in an edit and i'll look it over tomorrow :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except that the only body parts In-Universe!we know for sure it has at least parts of are hands and feet. Heck, we don't even know that. It could, for all IU!we know, just be something that exerts force in the shape of a handprint, and then leaves "footprints" in a manner similar to a rubber stamp. As the article stands, we have no evidence that there is anything to it aside from its "hands" and its "feet".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks mate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congratulations, -1 changed to +1 with the new interview logs :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh no those were completely seperate instances. The tub wasn't always in Ohio :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its footprints, the hand like imprints that appear around the subject's neck, and the way that the victim is killed as well as the way victims are pulled and almost tug-of-war'ed when guards try to pull people out. I feel like that's enough to justify an entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The description still needs serious editing. It reads like prose, not like science. Most glaringly, the only evidence of 1299-1's existence is its footprints. That is not enough evidence for a scientist to state that an actual person-like thing is there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Muuuuch better! The test logs added worlds of creep to this. Changed to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was a little leery on the "killer ghost that cannot be stopped" angle, but the test logs did it for me, especially that last one.

Oh, I noticed perhaps a continuity error: the tub was found in Ohio, but the water that comes out of it is from a New York municipality?

item.title: Re: Not Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Appreciate it guys :D That being said, i would love a domesticated 953. XD

item.title: Not Mod Post
item.content:encoded: IMO, expecting the Foundation to release people who shouldn't have been jailed in the first place (e.g. that guy) would be like expecting something like 953 to eventually be domesticated. There is a fundamental flaw in the reasoning involved and makes some bad assumptions about the nature of the Foundation and its mechanisms.

item.title: Mod post
item.content:encoded: Edrobot, judging by the author's reaction to your edit, you did not have his permission to change the fate of D-894. Don't make significant edits without asking, okay?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks mate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't voted before this but the changes have improved this a lot. I liked how the boyfriend freaked out over the tub. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually any gender will do, only in the case of continment breach is a female needed, but i'm updating the continment procedures to note that, thanks :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1299-1 continued to seize and drown personnel until it successfully killed a female D-class, at which point SCP-1299 deactivated.

From this, I take it that the SCP will only be "satisfied" if it murders a woman, yet this isn't noted anywhere else in the document. For example, the containment procedure should read:

Once a month, one female D-class is to enact Procedure F-30.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, but… there just isn't much to it that makes it interesting. Not a bad SCP, but not an outstanding one either… neutral vote for now.
